reasoning: GDP growth in Q4 2024 was +2.4%, avoiding a recession with the mildly negative Q1 2025 (-0.5%). Growth surged in Q2 2025 to +3.8%, and the US Treasury indicated on Nov 3, 2025 that economic growth solidified further in Q3 2025. Since there were no two consecutive negative quarters up to Q3 2025, the only remaining possibility for this question to resolve YES is if both Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 are negative. Given current robust economic data, the chance of this sudden reversal into a Q4 2025/Q1 2026 recession is extremely low (around 3%).
